taskDescription: Click on the key neuroanatomical terms in the text below.
textField:
The nervous system is divided into the *central* nervous system (brain and *spinal* cord) and the *peripheral* nervous system. The PNS includes 12 pairs of *cranial* nerves and 31 pairs of *spinal* nerves. The *autonomic* nervous system controls involuntary functions through its *sympathetic* and *parasympathetic* divisions.

*Neurons* are the functional units of the nervous system. A typical neuron has a cell body (*soma*), *dendrites* that receive signals, and an *axon* that transmits impulses. *Myelin* sheaths insulate axons and increase conduction velocity. In the CNS, *oligodendrocytes* produce myelin, while in the PNS, *Schwann* cells perform this function. *Astrocytes* maintain the *blood-brain* barrier and regulate the extracellular environment.

A spinal nerve forms from a *dorsal* root (carrying *sensory* fibres) and a *ventral* root (carrying *motor* fibres). An upper motor neuron lesion causes *spastic* paralysis with *hyperreflexia* and a positive *Babinski* sign. A lower motor neuron lesion causes *flaccid* paralysis with *atrophy* and *fasciculations*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Some key terms were missed.
Excellent! You identified the key neuroanatomical terms.
